```dafny
const PAD: nat := 4

// digits needed to write `value` (NumDigits(0) == 1)
function NumDigits(value: nat): nat { if value < 10 then 1 else 1 + NumDigits(value / 10) }

// a parsed entity id: kind tag, numeric value, and the width it is written at
datatype Id = Id(kind: string, value: nat, width: nat)

// input is wellformed if its width can actually hold its value
predicate Wellformed(x: Id) { x.width >= NumDigits(x.value) }

// (a) Implementation of Canonicalize
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id
{
  var minWidth := if NumDigits(x.value) < PAD then PAD else NumDigits(x.value);
  Id(x.kind, x.value, minWidth)
}

// (b) Specification lemma
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x);
    c.kind == x.kind &&
    c.value == x.value &&
    c.width >= PAD &&
    c.width >= NumDigits(c.value) &&
    (x.width >= PAD ==> c.width == x.width) &&
    (x.width < PAD ==> c.width == PAD)
{ }
```

The specification ensures:
1. **Kind and value are preserved** — the canonical form refers to the same entity
2. **Canonical width is at least PAD (4)** — minimum padding requirement
3. **Result is wellformed** — the width can hold the numeric value
4. **Idempotency on already-canonical ids** — if input width ≥ PAD, output width equals input width
5. **Padding for short widths** — if input width < PAD, output width becomes exactly PAD
